last_next_action.reason: execution queue is empty; wake the agent to synthesize or update the research agenda and advance all currently runnable safe project work from objective, evidence, human advice, and safety policy

recent_evidence.summary: Bridge-free trend comparison across six det=1.25 MHz running-autosave snapshots (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, and 14 balanced averages). Carrier and det+13C target-region amplitudes remain about percent scale but softened at 14 averages (carrier 0.94%, det+13C 1.02%), while old det=1 high/static and previous 1.9 MHz regions remain weak (0.28% and 0.23%). This still favors terminal focus on the det=1.25 carrier/high-sideband model over static old-high/1.9-MHz alternatives, but remains running-autosave provenance only; terminal 16-average review is required before any T2star or 13C conclusion.

c02 det=1.25 Ramsey 14-average autosave review and target-bin trend: Bridge-free 14-average autosave review of running det=1.25 MHz Ramsey job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey. The bridge stayed running at 14/16 averages with Final about 42.441 kcps, monitor active, no last_error, and stop_requested=false; no queue mutation was performed. Scan-order-aware drift found no flagged averages. Readout2/self-baseline target bins: direct 13C 0.54%, det-13C 0.82%, carrier 0.94%, det+13C 1.02%, old det=1 high/static 0.28%, static low 0.54%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.23%. Trend across 2,4,6,8,10,14 balanced averages keeps carrier/det+13C about percent-scale while old-high/static and previous 1.9 MHz stay weak. Nonterminal/provisional only; wait for terminal review.

Terminal 16-average det=1.25 MHz Ramsey review of reimage1804_c02 job nv23_ramsey_20260515_030822_auto_ramsey. The bridge completed cleanly at 06:41:21 EDT with pre-run TrackCenter 44.073 kcps, post-run Final 42.669 kcps, stop_requested=false, safe_shutdown_ok=true, 16 averages x 50000 reps over tau 0..10 us, snake scan order, and no drift flags. Readout2/self-baseline FFT targets: direct 13C 0.54%, det-13C 0.79%, carrier 1.00%, det+13C 1.01%, old det=1 high/static 0.39%, static low 0.46%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.23%. Across det=1.5/1.0/1.25 MHz, high-sideband amplitudes are 1.95/0.84/1.01%, leaving a weak det-shift-consistent 13C candidate but not a claim-grade Ramsey-only 13C observation. T2star is supported as short/few-us order, not a precise scalar.

Bridge-free weak-signal follow-up condition review after terminal det=1.25 Ramsey. The physical signature is a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate, but Ramsey remains ambiguous because target amplitudes are only about 0.5-1.0% and low-sideband/direct-Larmor/carrier support is incomplete. A non-blind follow-up candidate is auto__cpmg / CPMG.xml with N=8 and tau 0.45..1.60 us to cover both 1/(4 f13)=0.650 us and 1/(2 f13)=1.300 us conventions for f13=384.6 kHz. No bridge job was submitted; next bridge-touching work needs fresh intent, verifier, MATLAB advisory, queue-idle check, and current drift/window review.

c02 terminal det=1.25 Ramsey review and weak-13C follow-up condition review: Terminal det=1.25 MHz Ramsey for reimage1804_c02 completed cleanly at 06:41 EDT with healthy counts (44.073 kcps pre-run, 42.669 kcps post-run), 16 x 50000 reps over tau 0..10 us, snake scan order, and no drift flags. Readout2/self-baseline FFT target bins were direct 13C 0.54%, det-13C 0.79%, carrier 1.00%, det+13C 1.01%, old det=1 high/static 0.39%, static low 0.46%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.23%. Cross-det comparison keeps a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate (1.95/0.84/1.01% for det=1.5/1.0/1.25), but it is not a claim-grade Ramsey-only 13C observation. T2star is supported as short/few-us order, not a precise scalar. A bridge-free follow-up review identified a bounded CPMG tau-scan candidate but did not submit bridge work.

last_human_request.preview: Objective: Find a magnetic-field-aligned NV from image172647, then obtain a well-supported T2star and 13C conclusion. Start a project using the spatial range of <MATLAB_23C_ROOT>\SavedImages\3DXYZ-Image-2026-05-14-172647.mat as the initial search region. Identify an NV aligned to the magnetic field within that image range; if none is found, search different regions. Judge magnetic-field alignment using a strong-pi pulsed ODMR scan; if it does not show a clear usable resonance, reject
